profile.name: Nick Cook
roles: Author - The Hunt for Zero Point (2002)
Director, Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies
profile.summary: British aviation journalist who served as Aviation Editor at Jane's Defence Weekly for approximately 14 years, winning four Royal Aeronautical Society Aerospace Journalist of the Year Awards. His 2002 book 'The Hunt for Zero Point' documented a decade-long investigation into declassified 1950s electrogravitics research, Operation Paperclip-connected Nazi advanced weapons programs, and the alleged absorption of antigravity technology into classified US black programs. The book reached number one on Amazon's non-fiction chart and became a foundational text in UAP technology research circles. Cook's mainstream defense journalism credentials distinguish him from most writers in this field, though critics note his conclusions exceed the evidence he presents.
education: BA (Hons), Arabic and Islamic Studies, University of Exeter (1982)
early_career: Technology reporter for Interavia and International Defense Review, mid-1980s
Covered global aerospace and defense technology; gained access to top-secret US and former Soviet facilities during Jane's tenure
Wrote for the Financial Times and Wall Street Journal as a correspondent during the Jane's years
Expert analyst called upon by major media during the 1991 and 2003 Iraq conflicts

key_events.event: Graduated from the University of Exeter with a BA in Arabic and Islamic Studies. Began career as a technology reporter for Interavia and International Defense Review, covering aerospace and defense.

Joined Jane's Defence Weekly as Aviation Editor. Jane's is the world's leading defense intelligence publisher; the position gave Cook access to classified US and Soviet facilities and placed him among the most credentialed aerospace journalists in the world.
Published debut novel Angel, Archangel, followed by Aggressor in 1991, establishing him as a mainstream commercial novelist alongside his defense journalism.

Discovered a declassified 1950s technical report on electrogravitics while working at Jane's. The report documented that major US aerospace contractors including Douglas, Glenn Martin, General Electric, and Bell had been quietly funding gravity control research in the mid-1950s before the field vanished from public record around 1957-1958. This discovery initiated a decade-long investigation.
Produced and presented Billion Dollar Secret for the Discovery Channel - an investigation into classified US military spending and experimental aircraft as explanations for UFO sightings (IMDB: 6.9).

Departed Jane's Defence Weekly after approximately 14 years as Aviation Editor, having won four Royal Aeronautical Society Aerospace Journalist of the Year Awards across the Defence, Business, Technology, and Propulsion categories.

Published The Hunt for Zero Point: Inside the Classified World of Antigravity Technology (Broadway Books, US; Century Random House, UK). Reached number one on Amazon's non-fiction chart. Documents 1950s electrogravitics research, T. Townsend Brown's suppressed propulsion experiments, the alleged Nazi Bell (Die Glocke), SS General Hans Kammler's advanced weapons directorate, and the thesis that antigravity technology entered US black programs via Operation Paperclip. Primary physics source: Dr. Hal Puthoff.

Produced and presented UFOs: The Secret Evidence for the History Channel (US release title: An Alien History of Planet Earth). Investigated UFO sightings from the 1940s through 1990s in the context of classified military programs (IMDB: 7.5).

Co-wrote Barefoot Soldier: A Story of Extreme Valour with Johnson Beharry VC, the first British soldier awarded the Victoria Cross since the Falklands War. The book became a mainstream bestseller, demonstrating Cook's range beyond UAP subject matter.

Published The Grid, a near-future thriller about surveillance and perception manipulation, named a London Times Book of the Year. Launched NickCook.Works, a corporate storytelling and narrative strategy consultancy.

Submitted an essay to Robert Bigelow's BICS competition on evidence for survival of consciousness after death, winning a $20,000 prize. Bigelow subsequently invited Cook to become a director of the Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies.

Revised his view on the Nazi Bell (Die Glocke) in later interviews, stating he now believes it was more likely connected to a nuclear program than to antigravity research - a notable walk-back of one of the book's most dramatic claims. Current research interests have shifted toward consciousness studies and the intersection of UAP phenomena with consciousness.

credibility.note: Cook occupies an unusual position in UAP research. His mainstream defense journalism credentials - 14 years at Jane's, four Royal Aeronautical Society awards, access to classified facilities - distinguish him sharply from fringe self-published writers. The electrogravitics research he documented was real; the 1950s contractor programs were historically verifiable. Where his credibility frays is in the inferential leap from real but discontinued research to the conclusion that antigravity technology was secretly operationalized. His later revision of the Die Glocke claims is a mark of intellectual honesty rare in this field.
supporting: 14-year Aviation Editor at Jane's Defence Weekly, the world's leading defense intelligence publisher - among the most mainstream-credentialed authors in UAP-adjacent research
Four Royal Aeronautical Society Aerospace Journalist of the Year Awards across Defence, Business, Technology, and Propulsion categories

The 1950s electrogravitics contractor research he documented was real - Aviation Studies (International) reports, company funding programs, and T. Townsend Brown's Navy work are historically verifiable
Gained legitimate access to top-secret US and former Soviet facilities during his Jane's career, indicating institutional trust
Operation Paperclip itself is declassified confirmed history; the disputed element is whether antigravity specifically was among the transferred technologies
Book reached number one on Amazon's non-fiction chart, indicating broad mainstream readership
Later revised his Die Glocke claims based on updated research - demonstrates methodological honesty unusual in the UAP field
Bigelow Institute directorship connects him to the most credentialed private UAP research funding organization
contradicting: BA in Arabic and Islamic Studies - no engineering or physics background; technical analysis relies entirely on sources rather than independent expertise
Central anonymous source Marckus - described as an aerospace industry insider suggesting gravity shielding may be operational - is completely unverifiable
Die Glocke sourcing relies almost entirely on Igor Witkowski, whose account rests on claimed access to unverifiable Polish government documents
Cook himself later revised his Die Glocke assessment to nuclear rather than antigravity - undermining a major pillar of the book's central argument
Salon reviewer Kurt Kleiner described the book as a hodgepodge of information trawled from the Internet, other books and UAP enthusiasts, with conclusions built on circumstantial evidence
The Skeptic (UK) characterized it as a bizarre entertaining high-tech film-noir thriller, but not a history book
The inferential chain from discontinued 1950s research to covert operational antigravity technology is not supported by direct evidence
No independent confirmation of black-program antigravity development has emerged in the 20+ years since publication

associated_people.name: Dr. Hal Puthoff
associated_people.role: Physicist, Institute for Advanced Studies in Austin
associated_people.relationship: Puthoff served as Cook's primary physics expert for The Hunt for Zero Point, providing the theoretical framework connecting zero-point energy to antigravity propulsion. Cook describes Puthoff as his key scientific source for understanding how the physics of the alleged classified programs would work.

associated_people.name: Robert Bigelow
associated_people.role: Founder, BAASS / Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies
associated_people.relationship: Bigelow invited Cook to join the BICS board of directors following Cook's prize-winning essay in the 2020-2021 BICS consciousness competition, connecting Cook to one of the primary private funders of UAP research.

associated_people.name: Bob Lazar
associated_people.relationship: Lazar's claims about gravity-wave propulsion at S-4/Area 51 sit within the broader antigravity program landscape Cook investigates. Cook's research into 1950s electrogravitics provides historical context for the theoretical physics Lazar describes.

disclosures.title: Billion Dollar Secret
disclosures.notes: Written and presented by Cook. Investigated classified US military spending and experimental aircraft as explanations for UFO sightings. IMDB 6.9.

disclosures.title: The Hunt for Zero Point: Inside the Classified World of Antigravity Technology
disclosures.notes: Primary written disclosure. Reached number one on Amazon's non-fiction chart. Documents 1950s electrogravitics research, T. Townsend Brown, Die Glocke, and the thesis that antigravity technology entered US black programs via Operation Paperclip.

disclosures.title: UFOs: The Secret Evidence
disclosures.outlet: History Channel (US: An Alien History of Planet Earth)
disclosures.notes: Written and presented by Cook. Investigated UFO sightings from the 1940s through 1990s in the context of classified military programs. IMDB 7.5.

career_connections.relationship: Puthoff was Cook's primary physics source for The Hunt for Zero Point, providing the theoretical framework linking zero-point energy engineering to antigravity propulsion. Cook's investigative findings and Puthoff's theoretical work are deeply intertwined in the book's central argument.

Lazar's claims about gravity-wave propulsion at S-4/Area 51 are part of the antigravity program landscape Cook investigates. Cook's research into 1950s electrogravitics contractor programs provides historical context for the theoretical physics Lazar describes.

Bigelow invited Cook to join the BICS board of directors following his prize-winning essay in the 2020-2021 consciousness competition. This institutional connection links Cook to the primary private funder of UAP research in the modern era.
